mITyGuitar.changelog:
# mITyGuitar Desktop 0.0.1

## What's New
- Desktop application release for Windows
- Full featured guitar learning and practice application
- Chord mapping and song chart support
- Controller integration for interactive practice

## Downloads

### Windows Installers
- **MSI Package**: `mITyGuitar_0.0.1_x64_en-US.msi` - Windows Installer Package (recommended for most users)
- **NSIS Setup**: `mITyGuitar_0.0.1_x64-setup.exe` - Nullsoft Installer (alternative option)

Both installers include the complete application with all dependencies.

## System Requirements
- Windows 10/11 (64-bit)
- Audio device (headphones/speakers recommended)
- Optional: MIDI controller for enhanced interaction

## Installation
1. Download your preferred installer
2. Run the installer as administrator
3. Follow the setup wizard
4. Launch mITyGuitar from Start Menu or Desktop

## Issues & Support
If you encounter any issues, please report them on our [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/mITyGuitar/issues) page.

---
Generated on 2026-01-04 23:17:33 UTC

mITyStudio.changelog:
## mITyStudio v1.1.18

Automated Windows build of `master` @ 54d043e.

**Installing over a previous version updates in place** — your
workspace (projects, soundfonts, samples, voices) lives outside the
install directory and is preserved.

![Studio](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/mITyStudio/54d043ee7ae808f53c4aa66b77034fb4a5c0066c/docs/screenshots/studio.png)
![First-start guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/mITyStudio/54d043ee7ae808f53c4aa66b77034fb4a5c0066c/docs/screenshots/onboarding.png)
![Voices](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/mITyStudio/54d043ee7ae808f53c4aa66b77034fb4a5c0066c/docs/screenshots/voices.png)

### Changes
Agentic improvement loop + local learning: the studio hill-climbs a measured score and fits the user

Answers "make the agent learn and re-evaluate before delivering" — but the
honest, measured version, not the model grading itself.

Re-evaluate before delivering (arrangement_metrics + song_pipeline):
- score(): a composite 0-1 quality number from measurable dimensions
  (completeness, key adherence, dynamic arrangement, headroom/no-clipping,
  density sanity, full-song, has-ending, has-mix), plus the weakest
  dimensions so a fix can be targeted.
- The single critic pass is now an agentic LOOP (cap 3): measure → have the
  producer fix the WEAKEST dimensions → re-measure → keep the round only if
  the score climbed by MIN_GAIN, else revert it and stop. It also stops once
  the score clears QUALITY_TARGET (0.9). So the model can never make a song
  worse, and it does not burn LLM calls polishing a song that is already
  good — which, because the deterministic skeleton+mix+ending already scores
  ~0.99, is the common case. The loop earns its keep on genuinely weak output
  (off-key composer parts, an unfilled section). Score before/after and the
  round log go to the ledger.

Learn from what the user keeps/edits (preferences.py, local + deterministic):
- observe() runs ONLY on the user's

MusicAgent.changelog:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/89d1d0ee-8a6b-4b4d-81c8-eab136ed7c70)

✨ Features
- Modular Routing: Application routes have been split for improved readability and maintainability
- Azure Provider Integration: Initial support for Azure-based models has been added (temporarily disabled in current build)
- Creative Mode Playback: Added sample playback capability in the Creative Mode selection interface

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b0caefa-4c0d-433a-9233-584d69392668)


🧠 Logic & Architecture
- Service Refactoring: Core logic has been extracted and moved to dedicated service modules, paving the way for cleaner code and easier testing
- Centralized Configuration: API and Sonic Pi configurations are now centrally managed in config.json

🔧 Fixes & Improvements
- Path Handling: Corrected file path logic when creating new song files
- Import Cleanup: Removed unused imports and streamlined dependencies
- Metadata Handling: Ensured full sample metadata is available during song construction
- Config Corrections: Fixed typos and project root configuration issues

🧪 Known Limitations
- Azure model temporarily disabled while integration is being validated

PiBeat.changelog:
## 🎵 PiBeat

Desktop music live-coding — write Sonic Pi code and hear it instantly.

### Screenshots

| Editor & Waveform | Agent Chat |
|:-:|:-:|
| ![Editor](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/PiBeat/main/screenshots/editor.png) | ![Agent Chat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/PiBeat/main/screenshots/agent-chat.png) |

| Timeline View | Band Visualizer |
|:-:|:-:|
| ![Timeline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/PiBeat/main/screenshots/timeline.png) | ![Band Visualizer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/janvanwassenhove/PiBeat/main/screenshots/band-visualizer.png) |

### 📥 Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| 🪟 Windows | `.msi` / `.exe` | Windows 10+ |
| 🍎 macOS | `.dmg` (Universal) | Intel + Apple Silicon |
| 🐧 Linux | `.deb` / `.AppImage` | Ubuntu 22.04+ |

### ✨ Highlights
- Sonic Pi-compatible live coding editor with Monaco
- Real-time waveform visualization (wave, bars, lissajous)
- 42 synth types, 13 effects, built-in sample library
- AI assistant (OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini) for code generation
- Detachable panels, timeline view, band visualizer
- SuperCollider integration for professional sound quality

### 🚀 First-time Setup
1. Download and install for your platform
2. Open the editor and start writing Sonic Pi code
3. *(Optional)* Open Settings in Agent Chat to configure an AI provider

ghostsinthemachine.changelog:
**Ghosts in the Machine v1.0.5** — a haunted IT management sim. Run a paranormal IT
support company: build rooms, hire staff of questionable qualification, and route an
endless queue of absurd tickets while juggling money, client trust, technical debt
and the coffee supply.

▶ **Play in your browser:** https://janvanwassenhove.github.io/ghostsinthemachine/

## Download

| Platform | Installer |
| --- | --- |
| Windows (x64) | [`GhostsInTheMachine-1.0.5-win-x64.exe`](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/releases/download/v1.0.5/GhostsInTheMachine-1.0.5-win-x64.exe) |
| macOS (Apple Silicon) | [`GhostsInTheMachine-1.0.5-mac-arm64.dmg`](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/releases/download/v1.0.5/GhostsInTheMachine-1.0.5-mac-arm64.dmg) |
| macOS (Intel) | [`GhostsInTheMachine-1.0.5-mac-x64.dmg`](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/releases/download/v1.0.5/GhostsInTheMachine-1.0.5-mac-x64.dmg) |

The Windows build **updates itself**: if an older version is installed it downloads the
new one in the background and applies it on quit.

> **macOS is unsigned.** On first launch, right-click the app → *Open* to get past
> Gatekeeper. macOS auto-update is disabled until the app is signed and notarized.

## Screenshots

| Main menu | Campaign |
| --- | --- |
| ![Main menu](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/releases/download/v1.0.5/01-main-menu.png) | ![Campaign](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/releases/download/v1.0.5/02-campaign.png) |

![Gameplay](https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/releases/download/v1.0.5/03-gameplay.png)

## What's changed

- Cap rooms at two staff; a second worker reliably speeds up the room


**Full changelog:** https://github.com/janvanwassenhove/ghostsinthemachine/compare/v1.0.4...v1.0.5

scrum.changelog: # SCRUM v2.0.0 ## 🧠 New Language Feature: `#INTENT` SCRUM introduces **`#INTENT`**, enabling developers to express logic in natural language that is transformed into executable SCRUM code **at compile time**. ### Key characteristics - Natural language → SCRUM code - Executed at **compile time** - Powered by LLMs with **automatic provider fallback** - Generated code is validated and re-parsed - **No runtime AI dependency** ### Example ```scrum EPIC "StatisticsApp" USER STORY "ComputeStats" #INTENT I want to create a list of 5 numbers, calculate their sum and average, and display the results in a readable format. #END INTENT END OF USER STORY END OF EPIC StatisticsApp IS NEW StatisticsApp StatisticsApp::ComputeStats USING [] ``` During compilation, the #INTENT block is processed by the configured LLM provider. SCRUM generates valid SCRUM code, validates it, and executes it like any handwritten implementation. Same determinism. Less boilerplate. Zero AI magic at runtime. --- ## ☕ Platform Upgrade - **Upgraded to **Java 25** (LTS)** SCRUM now runs on the latest long-term support JVM, bringing improved performance, stability, and long-term maintainability. --- ## 📦 Fully Standalone SDK SCRUM is now distributed as a **complete, installable SDK**, allowing it to be used independently without manual JAR execution. ### What’s included - Cross-platform installers (Windows, Linux, macOS) - Global `scrum` CLI available on the PATH - No manual Java command invocation required - Bundled: - Examples - Documentation - CLI tooling SCRUM can now be installed, executed, and integrated like a first-class programming language. --- ## 🧰 Developer Experience Improvements - Enhanced CLI support: -
